title: SPLIT NME Kaworu and Shinji speculation
display_title: SPLIT NME Kaworu and Shinji speculation
wikitext:
[]: [SPLIT] NME Kaworu and Shinji speculation             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Oct 07, 2012 12:30 pm Split from this thread . -SSD Sailor Star Dust wrote: Not to get hopes up about new news, but Minna No Eva fan mentions the piano trailer could have some kind of connection to Shinji and Kaworu: http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-1802.html When asked "why a piano?", the producer (derping on names at the moment) in the magazine Gigazine claims "When you see the story, you will have an "Oh, I see" feeling about the content".

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Oct 07, 2012 4:17 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Not to get hopes up about new news, but Minna No Eva fan mentions the piano trailer could have some kind of connection to Shinji and Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Sun Oct 07, 2012 4:29 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: the Four Hands thing seems to imply a duet of some kind...

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Oct 07, 2012 4:38 pm ^ Too plain, I'm sure that it's way more grandiose than that, I think that it should be like "Kaworu and Shinji's love reshapes the world" or something.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Mon Oct 08, 2012 10:53 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: ^ Too plain, I'm sure that it's way more grandiose than that, I think that it should be like "Kaworu and Shinji's love reshapes the world" or something.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Oct 08, 2012 10:55 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: ^ Too plain, I'm sure that it's way more grandiose than that, I think that it should be like "Kaworu and Shinji's love reshapes the world" or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Tue Oct 09, 2012 12:53 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: ^ Too plain, I'm sure that it's way more grandiose than that, I think that it should be like "Kaworu and Shinji's love reshapes the world" or something.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Tue Oct 09, 2012 4:13 am Chrad wrote: If the 'point' of NME's ending is who Shinji gets with it's going to be a big fail.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Oct 09, 2012 9:51 am Q is certainly going to be Kaworu's big movie, (tough I say he's still doomed (because honestly, as much as I like him, that's his very role in the plot: to be doomed.. Such a "perfect" character would resolve the plot in 5 min if he didn't die, that's why he's allowed to be that way without turning into a Gary Stu, because he exists to have a profound impact on the protagonist.) and his death will be the final climax of the movie, preferably after he bonded with Shinji & co throuot the events in the middle of the movie so his death gets even more gutwrenching) that was clear from the beginning, since they bothered to give him a cameo as early as in 1.0. After all that setup, he's gonna have a big role. so the piano probably *does* refer to him. Inb4: Kaworu: Hi! I'm the Fifth Child and will be working with you from now on, nice to meet you all! So, please Introduce yourselves! Shinji: Hi, I'm Shinji. I'm 14 and my hobbies are, uh... Asuka: Hobbies? What hobbies! All he ever does is listed to his walkman and stare at the ceiling to brood! Kaworu. Really? I'm also very interested in music and philosophy! Let'*s practice our instruments together some time! cue Kaworu with the piano and Shinji with the cello. the song they play obviouly gets a tragic reprise after Kaworu dies.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Oct 09, 2012 10:38 am Kendrix wrote: tough I say he's still doomed

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Tue Oct 09, 2012 11:49 am I love how everyone here assumes NME Kaworu going to be exactly like NGE Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Oct 09, 2012 2:00 pm Na7e wrote: I love how everyone here assumes NME Kaworu going to be exactly like NGE Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Tue Oct 09, 2012 2:56 pm Na7e wrote: I love how everyone here assumes NME Kaworu going to be exactly like NGE Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Tue Oct 09, 2012 3:04 pm chaosakita wrote: Why not?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 09, 2012 4:31 pm I'm gonna split this Kaworu discussion into a speculation thread, but for now: Na7e wrote: I love how everyone here assumes NME Kaworu going to be exactly like NGE Kaworu.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Oct 09, 2012 5:20 pm Kendrix wrote: frankly, Anno would have to have had Tomatoes on his eyes not to notice the fallout that a certain colleague of his got for the EMK situation. DO NOT WANT.

[]:             Quote Post by Der Kommissar » Tue Oct 09, 2012 5:46 pm Anno's not one to shy away from something because the fans will get asspained. The Rebuild films have already considerably changed one character that was superficially the same, we'll only have to see if the same happens with Kaworu. Even NGE's Kaworu is shrouded in some fan confusion. You've got a goodly number that argue Kaworu was actively malevolent (though that doesn't really jive with EoE) or, if not intentionally, then by his nature.

[]:             Quote Post by StratoSakuya...AF » Tue Oct 09, 2012 5:59 pm cd603 wrote: Oh my god...that's how these movies end. Shinji gets with Kaworu. Think about it, he's the only one who ever truly loved him and didn't abandon him. (Yes he dies but by Shinji's hands) Plus, we all know Evangelion is nothing short of controversial and as much as I hate to say it, gay rights is one of the most controversial topics of our modern age. It wouldn't surprise me if this was the conclusion of the story, so you can thank Anno for the social commentary I guess.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Tue Oct 09, 2012 10:17 pm Kendrix wrote: Q is certainly going to be Kaworu's big movie, (tough I say he's still doomed (because honestly, as much as I like him, that's his very role in the plot: to be doomed.. Such a "perfect" character would resolve the plot in 5 min if he didn't die, that's why he's allowed to be that way without turning into a Gary Stu, because he exists to have a profound impact on the protagonist.)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 10, 2012 1:39 am cd603 wrote: gay rights is one of the most controversial topics of our modern age. It wouldn't surprise me if this was the conclusion of the story, so you can thank Anno for the social commentary I guess.
